elements.name: Description
texts: "In describing the seventeenth-century Fauna, Knox makes note of wolf and red deer at Lough Derg. 'The deer come some times from Barnesmore to this Estate but having no coverts they do not stay in it,' whereas 'the wolves are but rare in the country and seldom seen now.'"

elements.name: Source
texts: From the MS. Preserved at Castle Leslie, Co. Monaghan, 1666, Leslie, Shane, Saint Patrick's Purgatory: A Record from History and Literature, p. 83

elements.name: Publisher
texts: Burns Oats and Washbourne Ltd, London

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts: "In describing the seventeenth-century Fauna, Knox makes note of wolf and red deer at Lough Derg. 'The deer come some times from Barnesmore to this Estate but having no coverts they do not stay in it,' whereas 'the wolves are but rare in the country and seldom seen now.'"
